title: zamia pumila subsp. pygmaea
intro: Zamia pumila subsp. pygmaea is a small, dioecious cycad native to the island of Trinidad in the Caribbean. It is a type of plant used in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
